main_ingredients: mixed spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, garam masala)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Bawli Handi Mutton is approximately 250 g, comprising boneless mutton pieces in a rich spiced gravy served in or from a handi vessel.

nutrient_source.title: Bawli Handi (Mutton) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Bawli Handi (Mutton), cr_id G00010259, with confidence 0.95. All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed meat curry with vegetables
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bawli Handi Mutton. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous mixed meat curry entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is predominantly protein and fat with low carbohydrate (7.44 g/100g), consistent with low-GI classification. GL per serving calculated as (35 × 7.44 × 2.5) / 100 = 6.5 g for a 250 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 467 mg/100g — exceeds the 400 mg/100g threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 100–150 g per meal, request reduced-salt preparation, and avoid adding condiments; the 467 mg/100g sodium content can contribute substantially to the 1500–2000 mg daily sodium target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (7.44 g/100g) and low estimated GI (35); high protein (14.05 g/100g) supports glycaemic modulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Bawli Handi Mutton is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions (150–200 g) paired with low-GI accompaniments such as cauliflower rice or salad, as the low carbohydrate content and high protein minimise postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol 85 mg/100g, high sodium 467 mg/100g, and likely elevated saturated fat from cream and ghee
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally and in small portions (100–150 g), given the cholesterol load of 85 mg/100g, high sodium, and the saturated fat contribution from cream and ghee, which collectively elevate LDL and blood pressure risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mutton (red meat) is high in arachidonic acid; cream and ghee contribute pro-inflammatory saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise red meat frequency; if consuming this dish, request ghee substitution with olive oil, limit portion to 100 g, and monitor joint response to tomato (nightshade) content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bawli Handi Mutton is a slow-cooked Indian-origin boneless mutton dish prepared in a handi — a traditional deep, wide-mouthed cooking vessel — and served at Singaporean Indian restaurants. The dish features tender boneless mutton pieces braised in a rich, aromatic gravy made from onions, tomatoes, cream, ghee, ginger, garlic, and a blend of whole and ground spices including cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, and garam masala. The slow handi cooking method allows the spices to deeply penetrate the meat, yielding a dish with a complex, layered flavour profile: mildly spiced, slightly tangy from tomato, and richly creamy. The texture is tender and succulent with a thick, coating gravy. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 192 kcal, 14.05 g protein, 11.58 g fat, and 7.44 g carbohydrate, making it a protein-dense, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate dish. Sodium is elevated at 467 mg/100g, which is a concern for hypertension management. Cholesterol is 85 mg/100g. The estimated glycemic index is low (~35), making it suitable for blood glucose management when portioned appropriately. It is halal-certified in most Singapore establishments and is typically served as a main course at lunch or dinner, accompanied by naan, roti, or rice.
common_user_queries: Is Bawli Handi Mutton suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in Bawli Handi Mutton?
Is Bawli Handi Mutton high in sodium?
Can I eat Bawli Handi Mutton if I have hypertension?

The input description contained an error ('Specility bonless chicken dishs') — this dish is a mutton preparation, not chicken; the description has been corrected to reflect the actual food. 3. GI is an analogous estimate based on mixed meat curry entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Bawli Handi Mutton was found in any approved source. 4. All core macros (protein_g 14.05, fat_g 11.58, carbohydrate_g 7.44) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required.
